evidence: The paper uses ranger variable importance scoring to screen 12,806 DNA methylation probes down to 62 (discovery) and 38 (validation), and gene expression data down to 9 and 13 genes: "Ranger screened out 62 DNA methylation probes in the discovery phase according to variable importance score (VIS)." The core claim is "we identified 7 cancer-related genes and 12 DNA methylation probes as potentially associated with early-stage LUAD survival," which depends on this importance-based ranking. While the selected biomarkers are validated with Cox regression (FDR-corrected in discovery, p<0.05 in validation) and tested in independent cohorts, the paper provides no permutation importance, SHAP, PDP/ALE, or conditional importance analysis to verify that the importance ranking itself is accurate.
